description: One-pass review of crate secretx-hashicorp-vault (./secretx-hashicorp-vault). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Review scope: secretx-hashicorp-vault/ (lib.rs 630 lines, README 40 lines, Cargo.toml 23 lines). Build clean, clippy clean (--all-features --all-targets -D warnings). MSRV 1.75. Single recent commit '96cd4c7 fix: throttling errors, spawn_blocking, dispatch, URI scheme'. Decisions made autonomously per /review-rusty-crate rules; rationale captured in subsequent notes.
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — solid foundation with thoughtful security/zeroization story and good test coverage; principal risks are missing HTTP timeout (P1), missing namespace support for Vault Enterprise (P2), and the documented but contract-violating field put atomicity gap (P1). None are blockers; all are addressable in minor versions.
Correctness confidence: ~85% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: (1) the URL-percent-encoding finding (zew5.1) — admin trust model arguably makes this docs not bug, but Vault paths with spaces are common enough I am keeping it; (2) the field-put atomicity finding (zew5.3) — the trait contract language is explicit but the docstring footgun warning may be enough mitigation in practice. Uncertainty: did not exercise live Vault edge cases (sealed Vault, expired token with grace period, KV v1 vs v2 confusion, lease-based dynamic secrets). Cross-crate concerns (timeout pattern, Debug impl, BACKEND constant discipline) noted in per-crate issues but the systemic fix may belong in secretx-core.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code is broadly idiomatic Rust. Real findings: split_once over find+slice (zew5.16), is_some_and over .map().unwrap_or(false) (zew5.17). One taste-call: closure-vs-fn-pointer in .map() (zew5.18, opinion). No unsafe, no public macros, no lifetime annotations, no unnecessary clones, error handling uses ? consistently. Three other potential idiom flags (zew5.19/20/21) were filed and immediately self-cancelled because my own analysis concluded they were not real findings — kept the audit trail per the no-bikeshedding rule.
Rust idiom confidence: ~90% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: none — the idiom-level findings here are mechanical (clippy-tier) and verifiable by inspection. Uncertainty driven by: did not run cargo +nightly clippy with -W clippy::pedantic / -W clippy::nursery, which could surface additional minor flags (cast_lossless, redundant_else, etc.). Did not check cargo +msrv build to verify the is_some_and suggestion compiles on MSRV (workspace MSRV is 1.75; is_some_and stable since 1.70 — suggestion is safe).
title: Review: secretx-hashicorp-vault rusty pass
